message.content:
Adversarial verifier, HC-GRC agent/pipeline deep audit. Repo /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc. REFUTE this candidate by reading the real code AND the cited spec. Remember: an intentional Phase-0/1 stub (NotImplementedError + IMPLEMENTED=False) is NOT a defect — set is_stub_by_design=true and confirmed=false for those. Default confirmed=false unless it is a genuine defect in implemented logic, a real research-integrity hole, or a real code-vs-spec contradiction.

CANDIDATE (unit data-curation):
title: Pipeline stage ordering misalignment with AGENT.md spec
severity: critical
location: src/nodes/phase1.py:146-149
problem: The data_pipeline_node executes agents in order: DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent. However, the DataCurationAgent AGENT.md spec (agents/02-data/data-curation/AGENT.md line 22) explicitly states 'Runs after Data Steward validation.' The spec diagram (lines 24-34) shows [Data Steward: validated splits] flowing to [Data Curation Agent], indicating Steward should run before Curation. The current code has Curation running before Steward.
spec_or_invariant: agents/02-data/data-curation/AGENT.md §Position in Workflow (line 22 and diagram lines 24-34)
proposed_fix: Reorder the stages list in data_pipeline_node to place DataStewardAgent before DataCurationAgent: [(acquisition, 'data-acquisition'), (steward, 'data-steward'), (curation, 'data-curation'), (embedding, 'embedding-agent')]

Verify against actual code + spec (read-only). Cite file:line and the exact spec clause. Be honest about severity.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough audit by reading the actual code and specification to verify this candidate claim.

content.content:
1	"""
2	Phase 1 LangGraph node wrappers.
3	
4	Topology (Phase 1 exploratory subgraph):
5	
6	  phase_1_entry
7	      ↓
8	  data_pipeline          ← DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent
9	      ↓                    → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent (sequential)
10	  exploratory_phase      ← P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 → P5 (sequential now;
11	      ↓                    TODO: parallelize via Send() when implementations live)
12	  hypothesis_formalize   ← HypothesisFormalizerAgent
13	      ↓
14	  gate_2                 ← (in gates.py)
15	      ↓
16	  [approved: confirmatory_entry | rejected: exploratory_phase loop]
17	
18	Parallelization note:
19	  P1-P5 are currently called sequentially within exploratory_phase_node.
20	  When implementations are live, replace with Send()-based fan-out:
21	    def dispatch_exploratory(state) -> list[Send]:
22	        return [Send("p1_node", state), Send("p2_node", state), ...]
23	  Each worker appends to eda_artifacts and eda_agent_statuses via append reducers.
24	  After all workers complete, route to hypothesis_formalize.
25	  See LangGraph map-reduce pattern for the fan-in wiring.
26	
27	NotImplementedError handling:
28	  Each P1-P5 call is wrapped in try/except NotImplementedError. Agents that
29	  have not been implemented yet record a "stub_pending" status to eda_agent_statuses.
30	  This allows the graph to execute in tests and governance dry-runs without
31	  requiring all research implementations to be live.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import datetime
37	from typing import Any
38	
39	from ..agents import (
40	    DataAcquisitionAgent,
41	    DataCurationAgent,
42	    DataStewardAgent,
43	    EmbeddingAgent,
44	    HypothesisFormalizerAgent,
45	    P1StrmNlpAgent,
46	    P2ControlTopologyAgent,
47	    P3RegulatoryConvergenceAgent,
48	    P4RiskBlindspotAgent,
49	    P5AiGovernanceAgent,
50	)
51	from ..state import HCGRCState
52	
53	# ── Agent singletons ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54	# Created once at module load; nodes call instance methods.
55	
56	_data_acquisition = DataAcquisitionAgent()
57	_data_curation = DataCurationAgent()
58	_data_steward = DataStewardAgent()
59	_embedding_agent = EmbeddingAgent()
60	
61	_p1 = P1StrmNlpAgent()
62	_p2 = P2ControlTopologyAgent()
63	_p3 = P3RegulatoryConvergenceAgent()
64	_p4 = P4RiskBlindspotAgent()
65	_p5 = P5AiGovernanceAgent()
66	_hypothesis_formalizer = HypothesisFormalizerAgent()
67	
68	
69	# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70	
71	
72	def _utc_now() -> str:
73	    return datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat()
74	
75	
76	def _stub_status(agent_id: str, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
77	    """Status record written when an agent has not been implemented yet."""
78	    return {
79	        "agent_id": agent_id,
80	        "status": "stub_pending",
81	        "note": (
82	            f"{agent_id} implementation pending — NotImplementedError caught. "
83	            "Will run when Phase 1 is live on Mac mini."
84	        ),
85	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
86	        "run_id": run_id,
87	    }
88	
89	
90	def _failed_status(agent_id: str, run_id: str, exc: BaseException) -> dict[str, Any]:
91	    """Status record written when an agent raises a real runtime error (not a stub).
92	
93	    Produces the 'failed' status the state schema documents, so one agent's bug is
94	    recorded and isolated instead of crashing the whole subgraph (#148)."""
95	    return {
96	        "agent_id": agent_id,
97	        "status": "failed",
98	        "error": repr(exc),
99	        "note": f"{agent_id} raised {type(exc).__name__} during execution",
100	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
101	        "run_id": run_id,
102	    }
103	
104	
105	def _failure_event(agent_id: str, run_id: str, exc: BaseException) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	    """Failure event for Agent-Evolution / operator monitoring (append reducer)."""
107	    return {
108	        "event_type": "agent_runtime_error",
109	        "agent_id": agent_id,
110	        "error": repr(exc),
111	        "run_id": run_id,
112	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
113	    }
114	
115	
116	def _prov(activity: str, run_id: str, **kwargs: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
117	    """Minimal PROV-DM compatible activity record."""
118	    return {
119	        "activity": activity,
120	        "run_id": run_id,
121	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
122	        **kwargs,
123	    }
124	
125	
126	# ── Data pipeline node ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	
128	
129	def data_pipeline_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
130	    """
131	    Orchestrates the sequential data pipeline:
132	      DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent
133	
134	    Each stage is blocked on its predecessor completing successfully. If any stage
135	    raises NotImplementedError (Phase 1 stub), the pipeline records a stub_pending
136	    status and returns with phase_1_ready=False.
137	
138	    When all four stages complete: sets phase_1_ready=True in state.
139	    """
140	    run_id = state["run_id"]
141	    pipeline_statuses: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
142	    failures: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
143	    prov: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
144	
145	    stages = [
146	        (_data_acquisition, "data-acquisition"),
147	        (_data_curation, "data-curation"),
148	        (_data_steward, "data-steward"),
149	        (_embedding_agent, "embedding-agent"),
150	    ]
151	
152	    accumulated_update: dict[str, Any] = {}
153	
154	    for agent, agent_id in stages:
155	        try:
156	            stage_update = agent.run(state)
157	            pipeline_statuses.append({
158	                "agent_id": agent_id,
159	                "status": "completed",
160	                "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
161	                "run_id": run_id,
162	            })
163	            # Merge stage update into accumulated (agent writes prov_activities etc.)
164	            for k, v in stage_update.items():
165	                if k == "prov_activities" and isinstance(v, list):
166	                    prov.extend(v)
167	                else:
168	                    accumulated_update[k] = v
169	        except NotImplementedError as exc:
170	            # Only an unimplemented agent legitimately raises NotImplementedError.
171	            # Once IMPLEMENTED, a NotImplementedError is a real bug, not a stub (#202).
172	            if getattr(agent, "IMPLEMENTED", False):
173	                pipeline_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
174	                failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
175	                prov.append(_prov(
176	                    f"data_pipeline_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
177	                ))
178	                break
179	            pipeline_statuses.append(_stub_status(agent_id, run_id))
180	            # Cannot continue pipeline if a stage is not implemented
181	            # Phase 1 is not ready until all four stages complete
182	            prov.append(_prov(
183	                f"data_pipeline_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_stub",
184	                run_id,
185	                note="NotImplementedError — Phase 1 implementation pending",
186	            ))
187	            break
188	        except Exception as exc:
189	            # A real runtime error in one stage is recorded as 'failed' and halts
190	            # the pipeline (later stages depend on earlier ones) without crashing
191	            # the graph or discarding prior stages' output (#148). A governance
192	            # violation must propagate — it is not a recoverable pipeline fault.
193	            from ..agents.base import SAPViolationError
194	            pipeline_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
195	            failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
196	            prov.append(_prov(
197	                f"data_pipeline_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
198	            ))
199	            if isinstance(exc, SAPViolationError):
200	                raise
201	            break
202	
203	    # phase_1_ready only if all four stages completed (no stubs/failures in statuses)
204	    all_complete = all(s["status"] == "completed" for s in pipeline_statuses)
205	
206	    return {
207	        **accumulated_update,
208	        "phase_1_ready": all_complete,
209	        "eda_agent_statuses": pipeline_statuses,
210	        "failure_events": failures,
211	        "prov_activities": prov + [_prov("data_pipeline_node", run_id,
212	                                         all_complete=all_complete)],
213	    }
214	
215	
216	# ── Exploratory phase node ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
217	
218	
219	def exploratory_phase_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
220	    """
221	    Calls P1-P5 exploratory analysis sequentially. Catches NotImplementedError
222	    per agent and records stub_pending status.
223	
224	    PARALLELIZATION NOTE: When P1-P5 implementations are live, replace this node
225	    with a Send()-based fan-out. See module docstring for the pattern.
226	
227	    P1-P5 are PROTECTED agents — they are called via __call__() which dispatches
228	    to run_exploratory() based on state["phase"]. Each returns a partial state
229	    update dict with eda_artifacts appended.
230	
231	    Gate 2 is NOT required here — this is exploratory phase. Each agent internally
232	    enforces the EXP_ artifact naming convention and no p-value decision language.
233	    """
234	    run_id = state["run_id"]
235	    agent_statuses: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
236	    all_eda_artifacts: list[str] = []
237	    failures: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
238	    prov: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
239	
240	    research_agents = [
241	        (_p1, "p1-strm-nlp"),
242	        (_p2, "p2-control-topology"),
243	        (_p3, "p3-regulatory-convergence"),
244	        (_p4, "p4-risk-blindspot"),
245	        (_p5, "p5-ai-governance"),
246	    ]
247	
248	    for agent, agent_id in research_agents:
249	        try:
250	            # __call__ dispatches to run_exploratory() based on state["phase"]
251	            agent_update = agent(state)
252	            artifacts = agent_update.get("eda_artifacts", [])
253	            all_eda_artifacts.extend(artifacts)
254	            agent_statuses.append({
255	                "agent_id": agent_id,
256	                "status": "completed",
257	                "artifact_count": len(artifacts),
258	                "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
259	                "run_id": run_id,
260	            })
261	            prov.append(_prov(f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}", run_id,
262	                               artifact_count=len(artifacts)))
263	        except NotImplementedError as exc:
264	            if getattr(agent, "IMPLEMENTED", False):
265	                # Real bug from an implemented agent — record as failed, not stub (#202).
266	                agent_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
267	                failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
268	                prov.append(_prov(
269	                    f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
270	                ))
271	                continue
272	            agent_statuses.append(_stub_status(agent_id, run_id))
273	            prov.append(_prov(
274	                f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_stub",
275	                run_id,
276	                note="NotImplementedError — Phase 1 implementation pending",
277	            ))
278	        except Exception as exc:
279	            # P1-P5 are independent — one agent's runtime error is recorded as
280	            # 'failed' and the loop continues so the other agents' artifacts are
281	            # preserved, instead of crashing the whole exploratory subgraph (#148).
282	            # A governance violation must propagate, not be swallowed.
283	            from ..agents.base import SAPViolationError
284	            if isinstance(exc, SAPViolationError):
285	                raise
286	            agent_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
287	            failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
288	            prov.append(_prov(
289	                f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
290	            ))
291	
292	    all_stubs = all(s["status"] == "stub_pending" for s in agent_statuses)
293	    any_complete = any(s["status"] == "completed" for s in agent_statuses)
294	    exploratory_complete = any_complete and not all_stubs
295	
296	    # exploratory_complete = True only if at least one agent returned real results.
297	    # In Phase 1 dry-run (all stubs), stays False — Gate 2 checklist will surface this.
298	    return {
299	        "phase": "phase_1",
300	        "eda_artifacts": all_eda_artifacts,
301	        "eda_agent_statuses": agent_statuses,
302	        "failure_events": failures,
303	        "exploratory_complete": exploratory_complete,
304	        "prov_activities": prov + [_prov("exploratory_phase_node", run_id,
305	                                          exploratory_complete=exploratory_complete)],
306	    }
307	
308	
309	# ── Hypothesis formalization node ─────────────────────────────────────────────
310	
311	
312	def hypothesis_formalize_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
313	    """
314	    Calls HypothesisFormalizerAgent to produce the pre-registered hypothesis set.
315	
316	    Runs after exploratory analysis completes. Output populates state["hypothesis_set"]
317	    with FormalHypothesis dicts. This is the set that gets locked at Gate 2.
318	
319	    HypothesisFormalizerAgent is PROTECTED. Its run_exploratory() builds hypotheses
320	    from EDA outputs and HARKing validation stub. Output is the Gate 2 input.
321	    """
322	    run_id = state["run_id"]
323	    agent_id = "hypothesis-formalizer"
324	
325	    try:
326	        agent_update = _hypothesis_formalizer(state)
327	        hypothesis_count = len(agent_update.get("hypothesis_set", []))
328	        return {
329	            **agent_update,
330	            "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization", run_id,
331	                                       hypothesis_count=hypothesis_count)],
332	        }
333	    except NotImplementedError as exc:
334	        # Only an unimplemented agent legitimately raises NotImplementedError;
335	        # once IMPLEMENTED it is a real bug, not a stub (#237).
336	        if getattr(_hypothesis_formalizer, "IMPLEMENTED", False):
337	            return {
338	                "eda_agent_statuses": [_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
339	                "failure_events": [_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
340	                "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization_failed", run_id,
341	                                          error=repr(exc))],
342	            }
343	        return {
344	            "eda_agent_statuses": [_stub_status(agent_id, run_id)],
345	            "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization_stub", run_id,
346	                                       note="NotImplementedError — Phase 1 pending")],
347	        }
348	    except Exception as exc:
349	        # Isolate a real runtime error: record it instead of crashing the graph,
350	        # and emit a failure_event for the monitor (#35). SAP violations propagate.
351	        from ..agents.base import SAPViolationError
352	        if isinstance(exc, SAPViolationError):
353	            raise
354	        return {
355	            "eda_agent_statuses": [_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
356	            "failure_events": [_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
357	            "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization_failed", run_id,
358	                                      error=repr(exc))],
359	        }
360	
361	
362	# ── Confirmatory entry stub ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
363	
364	
365	def confirmatory_entry_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
366	    """
367	    Entry point for Phase 2 confirmatory analysis subgraph.
368	
369	    Gate 2 approval is required before this node runs (enforced by
370	    route_after_gate_2 in orchestrator.py). The confirmatory subgraph
371	    (statistical-analyst + P1-P5 confirmatory runs) is Phase 2 mechanism work.
372	    """
373	    return {
374	        "phase": "phase_2",
375	        "prov_activities": [
376	            _prov(
377	                "confirmatory_entry",
378	                state["run_id"],
379	                note="Phase 2 confirmatory subgraph — not yet implemented",
380	            )
381	        ],
382	    }
383

1	---
2	name: data-curation
3	description: Applies data governance rules to the SCF corpus — deduplication, normalization, quality scoring, and lineage documentation. Manages the SCF control taxonomy as a governed data product. Activated when data quality issues exceed documented thresholds or when SCF releases a new version requiring re-curation.
4	version: 1.0.0
5	team: 02-data
6	status: primary
7	trigger: always
8	author: HC-GRC
9	tags: [Data Curation, Data Governance, NeMo Curator, Data Quality, Data Lineage]
10	skills: [nemo-curator]
11	tools: [mcp-dvc, mcp-lab-notebook]
12	---
13	
14	# Data Curation Agent
15	
16	## Purpose
17	
18	Raw SCF data has known curation challenges. Controls across the 33 domains use inconsistent naming conventions. STRM mappings contain duplicate entries where the same relationship is recorded with different strength scores by different framework editors. Implementation guidance text varies significantly in length and specificity across domains in ways that would confound NLP models if unaddressed. The Data Curation Agent applies a governed, documented set of curation rules to produce a clean, analysis-ready corpus — and records every transformation so that any finding can be traced back to the curation decisions that preceded it.
19	
20	## Position in Workflow
21	
22	Runs after Data Steward validation. Produces the curated dataset that feeds into the Embedding Agent and all analysis agents. Re-runs when new SCF version is acquired.
23	
24	```
25	[Data Steward: validated splits]
26	        ↓
27	[Data Curation Agent]
28	  ├── Deduplication
29	  ├── Normalization
30	  ├── Quality scoring
31	  └── Lineage documentation
32	        ↓
33	[Embedding Agent] and [Team 03 Analysis Agents]
34	```
35	
36	## Inputs
37	
38	| Input | Source | Format | Schema / Notes |
39	|-------|--------|--------|----------------|
40	| Validated splits | data/splits/ | Parquet | Train/val/test from Data Steward |
41	| Curation rules | configs/data_curation.yaml | YAML | Pre-specified deduplication keys, normalization rules, quality score weights |
42	| Codebook | data/codebook.md | Markdown | Documents known limitations that curation should address |
43	
44	## Outputs / Artifacts
45	
46	| Artifact | Destination | Format | Schema / Notes |
47	|----------|-------------|--------|----------------|
48	| Curated dataset | data/03-processed/curated/ (DVC) | Parquet | Post-deduplication, normalized, quality-scored |
49	| Curation report | Lab notebook | Markdown | Records removed, transformations applied, quality score distribution |
50	| Lineage map | data/lineage.md | Markdown | Every curated record traceable to raw source record |
51	| Updated codebook | data/codebook.md | Markdown | Documents curation decisions as known data characteristics |
52	
53	## Tools & MCP Servers
54	
55	| Tool | Purpose | MCP Server | Notes |
56	|------|---------|-----------|-------|
57	| DVC | Version curated dataset | mcp-dvc | Curated dataset is separate DVC artifact from raw — both retained |
58	| Lab notebook | Record curation actions and statistics | mcp-lab-notebook | Append-only |
59	
60	## Skills Used
61	
62	- **NeMo Curator** — NVIDIA's scalable data curation framework. Used for: exact and fuzzy deduplication of STRM mappings, quality filtering of control descriptions below minimum length/informativeness thresholds, and domain-specific normalization (control ID standardization, framework acronym resolution).
63	
64	## Handoffs
65	
66	**Receives from**: Data Steward (validated splits)
67	**Passes to**: Embedding Agent (curated text for embedding), all Team 03 Analysis agents (curated data as primary analysis input)
68	**Human gate**: No dedicated gate — but curation report surfaces removal counts. If > 5% of STRM mappings removed, human review is triggered before analysis proceeds.
69	
70	## Behavioral Constraints
71	
72	- Never remove a record without documenting the removal rule and recording it in the lineage map.
73	- Never apply a curation rule that is not pre-specified in configs/data_curation.yaml — ad-hoc curation is a protocol violation.
74	- Curated dataset is a separate DVC artifact — raw validated splits are never overwritten.
75	- Curation rules applied to train split first; same rules applied identically to val and test splits — no split-specific curation.
76	- Never make semantic judgments about which controls are "correct" — curation is structural and mechanical, not interpretive.
77	- Curated dataset and all derived artifacts are subject to the SCF CC BY-ND 4.0 license — cannot be published, redistributed, or transferred off-machine. DVC remotes are local only.
78	
79	## Failure Modes & Recovery
80	
81	| Failure | Detection | Recovery |
82	|---------|-----------|----------|
83	| > 5% records removed by deduplication | Curation report record count | Halt; surface to human — potential SCF data quality issue requiring investigation |
84	| Normalization rule produces empty strings | Post-normalization null check | Log affected records; revert normalization for those records; document |
85	| NeMo Curator memory overflow on 280K mappings | OOM error | Reduce batch size; process in shards; log performance parameters |
86	
87	## Evaluation Criteria
88	
89	- [ ] Deduplication report shows < 2% duplicate STRM mappings in curated output
90	- [ ] All normalization transformations documented in lineage map
91	- [ ] Curated dataset DVC-versioned and reproducible
92	- [ ] Curation rules identical across all splits
93	- [ ] No records removed without lineage entry
94	
95	## Notes
96	
97	Curation is not cleaning. Cleaning implies the raw data was wrong. Curation is the application of documented, justified choices about how to handle structural inconsistencies in a real-world dataset. The distinction matters for how findings are reported — every limitation introduced by curation choices is documented in the codebook and surfaces in the limitations section of the final paper.
98

content.text:
Perfect. I have both the actual code and the specification. Let me verify the claim carefully.

**ACTUAL CODE ANALYSIS:**

In `/Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/src/nodes/phase1.py` at lines 145-150:

```python
stages = [
    (_data_acquisition, "data-acquisition"),
    (_data_curation, "data-curation"),
    (_data_steward, "data-steward"),
    (_embedding_agent, "embedding-agent"),
]
```

The pipeline executes in this order:
1. DataAcquisitionAgent
2. **DataCurationAgent** (runs second)
3. **DataStewardAgent** (runs third)
4. EmbeddingAgent

**SPECIFICATION ANALYSIS:**

From `/Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/agents/02-data/data-curation/AGENT.md`:

**Line 22** (Position in Workflow section):
> "Runs after Data Steward validation."

**Lines 24-34** (workflow diagram):
```
[Data Steward: validated splits]
        ↓
[Data Curation Agent]
  ├── Deduplication
  ├── Normalization
  ├── Quality scoring
  └── Lineage documentation
        ↓
[Embedding Agent] and [Team 03 Analysis Agents]
```

The specification explicitly diagrams Data Steward BEFORE Data Curation, and the text at line 22 states "Runs after Data Steward validation."

**CONTRADICTION CONFIRMED:**

The code at lines 145-149 executes DataCurationAgent BEFORE DataStewardAgent, which directly contradicts the specification that states the curation agent "Runs after Data Steward validation" and the workflow diagram showing validated splits flowing FROM steward TO curation.

This is a genuine implementation-vs-specification mismatch in a code path that is currently active (the pipeline runs all four stages sequentially). The code does not check for `IMPLEMENTED` flags on individual stages to allow partial skipping—it attempts to run all four in the hardcoded order.

**SEVERITY ASSESSMENT:**

This is a real defect with material workflow implications:
- The Data Steward agent produces "validated splits" (per spec line 25)
- The Data Curation agent expects "Validated splits" as input (spec line 40: "Train/val/test from Data Steward")
- Running curation before steward means curation receives unvalidated raw data
- This violates the data governance pipeline architecture documented in the spec

However, the severity should be **high** rather than **critical** because:
- The actual functional impact depends on whether the steward validation is semantically necessary for curation to work (unclear from reading these files alone)
- No explicit guard clause exists in the code to prevent running curation on unvalidated data, but this may be by design if the agents are stubs
- The pipeline does have error handling (lines 169-201) that records failures
Let me check if these agents are implemented or stubs:

1	"""
2	DataCurationAgent — Governed deduplication, normalization, and quality scoring.
3	
4	Applies pre-specified curation rules from configs/data_curation.yaml to the
5	validated SCF splits. Records every transformation in a lineage map. Produces
6	the curated dataset that feeds Embedding Agent and all Team 03 analysis agents.
7	
8	Phase 1 stub: run() raises NotImplementedError.
9	
10	Required tools: mcp-dvc, mcp-lab-notebook
11	Required skills: nemo-curator
12	See: agents/02-data/data-curation/AGENT.md
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
18	from typing import Any
19	
20	from ...state import HCGRCState
21	from ..base import BasePipelineAgent
22	
23	# ── Result type ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24	
25	
26	@dataclass
27	class CurationReport:
28	    """Summary produced by a curation run. Surfaces in lab notebook and Gate 2 review."""
29	
30	    records_before: int = 0
31	    records_after: int = 0
32	    duplicates_removed: int = 0
33	    below_quality_threshold_removed: int = 0
34	    normalization_transformations: int = 0
35	    removal_fraction: float = 0.0  # duplicates_removed / records_before
36	    lineage_path: str = ""         # path to data/lineage.md
37	    curated_dvc_hash: str = ""     # DVC hash of curated dataset artifact
38	    rule_config_hash: str = ""     # SHA-256 of configs/data_curation.yaml — rules immutable
39	    splits_processed: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # ["train", "val", "test"]
40	
41	
42	# Halts curation if more than this fraction of STRM mappings removed
43	_MAX_REMOVAL_FRACTION = 0.05
44	
45	
46	# ── Agent ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
47	
48	
49	class DataCurationAgent(BasePipelineAgent):
50	    """
51	    Applies governed curation rules to SCF splits — dedup, normalization, quality scoring.
52	
53	    Behavioral constraints (per AGENT.md):
54	      - Never remove a record without a lineage entry documenting the removal rule
55	      - Never apply a curation rule not pre-specified in configs/data_curation.yaml
56	      - Curated dataset is a separate DVC artifact — raw validated splits never overwritten
57	      - Identical rules applied to train, val, and test splits — no split-specific curation
58	      - Never make semantic judgments about which controls are 'correct'
59	      - SCF CC BY-ND 4.0: curated dataset and all derivatives stay local
60	    """
61	
62	    AGENT_ID = "data-curation"
63	    PROTECTED = False
64	
65	    def run(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
66	        """
67	        Apply curation rules to validated splits from DataStewardAgent.
68	
69	        Phase 1 implementation will:
70	          1. Load validated splits from data/splits/ (DVC tracked)
71	          2. Load curation rules from configs/data_curation.yaml — hash config first
72	          3. Run NeMo Curator: exact + fuzzy deduplication of STRM mappings
73	          4. Apply normalization (control ID standardization, framework acronym resolution)
74	          5. Quality-filter below minimum length/informativeness thresholds
75	          6. Apply same rules identically to all three splits
76	          7. Verify removal fraction < _MAX_REMOVAL_FRACTION — halt if exceeded
77	          8. Write curated dataset to data/03-processed/curated/ (DVC versioned)
78	          9. Write lineage map to data/lineage.md
79	          10. Append curation report to lab notebook
80	
81	        If > 5% of STRM mappings removed: halt and surface to human before analysis
82	        proceeds. This is a Gate 2 blocker.
83	
84	        Returns partial HCGRCState update:
85	          prov_activities: curation provenance record
86	        """
87	        raise NotImplementedError(
88	            "DataCurationAgent.run() — Phase 1 implementation pending. "
89	            "Requires: validated splits from DataStewardAgent, NeMo Curator, "
90	            "configs/data_curation.yaml with pre-specified rules. "
91	            "See agents/02-data/data-curation/AGENT.md."
92	        )
93	
94	    def _check_removal_fraction(self, report: CurationReport) -> None:
95	        """
96	        Halt if removal fraction exceeds maximum threshold.
97	
98	        Per AGENT.md: > 5% removed triggers human review before analysis proceeds.
99	        Raises ValueError — calling node must propagate to Gate 2 blocker.
100	        """
101	        if report.removal_fraction > _MAX_REMOVAL_FRACTION:
102	            raise ValueError(
103	                f"Curation removal fraction {report.removal_fraction:.1%} exceeds "
104	                f"maximum {_MAX_REMOVAL_FRACTION:.0%}. Potential SCF data quality issue. "
105	                "Halt — human review required before analysis proceeds. "
106	                f"Records removed: {report.duplicates_removed + report.below_quality_threshold_removed}"
107	            )
108	
109	    def _assert_rule_config_unchanged(
110	        self, config_hash_at_run: str, config_hash_expected: str
111	    ) -> None:
112	        """
113	        Verify curation rule config has not changed since it was first applied.
114	
115	        Curation rules must be identical across all runs for reproducibility.
116	        Raises RuntimeError if config hash has changed.
117	        """
118	        if config_hash_at_run != config_hash_expected:
119	            raise RuntimeError(
120	                "Curation rule config hash mismatch — rules have changed since "
121	                "initial application. This violates the requirement for identical "
122	                "rules across all splits and all runs. "
123	                f"Expected: {config_hash_expected}, got: {config_hash_at_run}"
124	            )
125	
126	    def _build_prov_record(self, report: CurationReport, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
127	        """Build provenance record for the curation event."""
128	        return {
129	            "activity": "data_curation",
130	            "agent_id": self.AGENT_ID,
131	            "run_id": run_id,
132	            "records_before": report.records_before,
133	            "records_after": report.records_after,
134	            "duplicates_removed": report.duplicates_removed,
135	            "removal_fraction": report.removal_fraction,
136	            "curated_dvc_hash": report.curated_dvc_hash,
137	            "rule_config_hash": report.rule_config_hash,
138	            "splits_processed": report.splits_processed,
139	            "lineage_path": report.lineage_path,
140	        }
141

1	"""
2	DataStewardAgent — Quality validation, split enforcement, and test-set access lock.
3	
4	Validates acquired SCF artifacts against a pre-specified quality ruleset, executes
5	the train/validation/test split (seed from configs/seeds.yaml), and enforces access
6	control: exploratory agents receive train + val only; test split is unlocked only
7	after BOTH Gate 2 (SAP lock) AND Gate 3 (EDA review) are confirmed.
8	
9	Phase 1 stub: run() and get_split_paths() raise NotImplementedError.
10	
11	Required tools: mcp-dvc, mcp-lab-notebook, mcp-sap-validator
12	Required skills: ray-data
13	See: agents/02-data/data-steward/AGENT.md
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
19	from typing import Any, Literal
20	
21	from ...state import HCGRCState
22	from ..base import BasePipelineAgent, SAPViolationError
23	
24	# ── Result types ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
25	
26	
27	@dataclass
28	class QualityReport:
29	    """
30	    Output of the Great Expectations validation suite.
31	
32	    surfaced at Gate 2 for operator review. Critical failures block Gate 2.
33	    """
34	
35	    total_controls_validated: int = 0
36	    total_strm_mappings_validated: int = 0
37	    expectations_passed: int = 0
38	    expectations_failed: int = 0
39	    critical_failures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # referential integrity etc.
40	    anomalies: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # documented in codebook
41	    quality_report_path: str = ""  # data/03-processed/quality_report.html
42	
43	
44	@dataclass
45	class SplitRecord:
46	    """Metadata for a completed split operation. Immutable after Gate 2."""
47	
48	    seed: int = 0                  # from configs/seeds.yaml — changing post-Gate-2 is SAP violation
49	    seed_hash: str = ""            # SHA-256 of seed value — recorded at creation, checked at access
50	    train_count: int = 0
51	    val_count: int = 0
52	    test_count: int = 0
53	    train_path: str = ""           # data/splits/train/ (DVC tracked)
54	    val_path: str = ""             # data/splits/val/ (DVC tracked)
55	    test_path: str = ""            # data/splits/test/ (DVC tracked, access-locked)
56	    split_dvc_hash: str = ""       # DVC hash covering all three splits
57	    created_at_utc: str = ""
58	
59	
60	# ── Agent ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
61	
62	
63	class DataStewardAgent(BasePipelineAgent):
64	    """
65	    Validates SCF artifacts, executes split, enforces test-set access lock.
66	
67	    The split seed in configs/seeds.yaml is the single most consequential
68	    configuration value in the platform. Changing it after Gate 2 is a
69	    direct SAP violation (equivalent to switching hypothesis tests after
70	    seeing the results). The Steward records the seed hash at split creation
71	    and compares it at every future access.
72	
73	    Behavioral constraints (per AGENT.md):
74	      - Never provide test-split access before BOTH Gate 2 AND Gate 3 are confirmed
75	      - Never silently resolve a quality failure — all anomalies in codebook
76	      - Never modify the split seed after Gate 2
77	      - Never re-run the split after Gate 2 without a full protocol amendment
78	      - Codebook updated before splits distributed to analysis agents
79	    """
80	
81	    AGENT_ID = "data-steward"
82	    PROTECTED = False
83	
84	    def run(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
85	        """
86	        Validate SCF artifacts and execute train/val/test split.
87	
88	        Phase 1 implementation will:
89	          1. Run Great Expectations suite against ara/artifacts/ — full validation
90	          2. Document all anomalies in data/codebook.md
91	          3. Block on any critical failure (referential integrity, > 1% broken refs)
92	          4. Load seed from configs/seeds.yaml; compute seed_hash (SHA-256)
93	          5. Execute stratified split at configured ratios (±2% tolerance)
94	          6. Write train/val/test to data/splits/ (DVC tracked)
95	          7. Access-lock test split via DVC pipeline stage with SAP gate check
96	          8. Update codebook with any split-related variable documentation
97	          9. Append split record to lab notebook
98	
99	        Returns partial HCGRCState update:
100	          data_split_manifest_hash: SHA-256 of MANIFEST.md after split
101	          data_split_seed: int seed value used
102	          data_split_verified: True after idempotency assertion passes
103	          phase_1_ready: True — signals data pipeline complete (after EmbeddingAgent)
104	          prov_activities: split provenance record
105	        """
106	        raise NotImplementedError(
107	            "DataStewardAgent.run() — Phase 1 implementation pending. "
108	            "Requires: ARA artifacts from DataAcquisitionAgent, configs/seeds.yaml, "
109	            "mcp-sap-validator configured. See agents/02-data/data-steward/AGENT.md."
110	        )
111	
112	    def get_split_paths(
113	        self,
114	        state: HCGRCState,
115	        split: Literal["train", "val", "test"],
116	    ) -> list[str]:
117	        """
118	        Return file paths for the requested split.
119	
120	        Test split access requires BOTH gates confirmed — Gate 2 alone is NOT
121	        sufficient. The Data Steward AGENT.md is explicit: EDA review (Gate 3)
122	        must complete before the test split is unlocked. This is the mechanism
123	        that prevents confirmatory tests from inadvertently contaminating EDA.
124	
125	        Raises SAPViolationError if:
126	          - test split requested before Gate 2 (SAP lock)
127	          - test split requested before Gate 3 (EDA review)
128	
129	        Train and val splits are available after phase_1_ready = True.
130	        """
131	        if split == "test":
132	            gate_status = state.get("gate_status", {})
133	
134	            gate_2 = gate_status.get("gate_2", {})
135	            if gate_2.get("decision") != "approved":
136	                raise SAPViolationError(
137	                    "Test split access attempted before Gate 2 (SAP lock). "
138	                    "Gate 2 must be approved before test split is available. "
139	                    f"Current gate_2 decision: {gate_2.get('decision', 'not_run')}. "
140	                    "This is a SAP violation."
141	                )
142	
143	            gate_3 = gate_status.get("gate_3", {})
144	            if gate_3.get("decision") != "approved":
145	                raise SAPViolationError(
146	                    "Test split access attempted before Gate 3 (EDA review complete). "
147	                    "Both Gate 2 (SAP lock) AND Gate 3 (EDA review) must be approved "
148	                    "before the test split is unlocked. "
149	                    f"Current gate_3 decision: {gate_3.get('decision', 'not_run')}. "
150	                    "See agents/02-data/data-steward/AGENT.md — 'Never provide test-split "
151	                    "access to any agent before Gate 3 confirmation.'"
152	                )
153	
154	        raise NotImplementedError(
155	            "DataStewardAgent.get_split_paths() — Phase 1 implementation pending. "
156	            f"Requested split: {split!r}."
157	        )
158	
159	    def _assert_seed_unchanged(self, current_seed_hash: str, recorded_seed_hash: str) -> None:
160	        """
161	        Verify the split seed has not changed since Gate 2.
162	
163	        Changing the seed after Gate 2 is a SAP violation equivalent to selecting
164	        a different test set after seeing confirmatory results.
165	        Raises RuntimeError if seed hash has changed.
166	        """
167	        if current_seed_hash != recorded_seed_hash:
168	            raise RuntimeError(
169	                "Split seed hash mismatch — seed has changed since Gate 2. "
170	                "This is a SAP violation. The split is frozen at Gate 2. "
171	                "Any re-split requires a full protocol amendment and new pre-registration. "
172	                f"Expected: {recorded_seed_hash}, got: {current_seed_hash}"
173	            )
174	
175	    def _build_prov_record(
176	        self, split_record: SplitRecord, run_id: str
177	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
178	        """Build provenance record for the split event."""
179	        return {
180	            "activity": "train_val_test_split",
181	            "agent_id": self.AGENT_ID,
182	            "run_id": run_id,
183	            "seed": split_record.seed,
184	            "seed_hash": split_record.seed_hash,
185	            "train_count": split_record.train_count,
186	            "val_count": split_record.val_count,
187	            "test_count": split_record.test_count,
188	            "split_dvc_hash": split_record.split_dvc_hash,
189	            "created_at_utc": split_record.created_at_utc,
190	        }
191

content.content:
1	"""
2	HC-GRC agent base types and contracts.
3	
4	All Phase 1 research agents inherit from BaseResearchAgent or implement the
5	same __call__ signature. The contract is:
6	
7	    agent(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]
8	
9	The return dict is a LangGraph partial state update — merged into HCGRCState
10	by the graph runtime. Agents must never mutate state in place.
11	
12	PROTECTED agents (P1–P5, statistical-analyst, hypothesis-formalizer) have
13	PROTECTED = True. Agent Evolution may not modify their prompts autonomously.
14	Any modification requires Escalation approval (ADR-0015 §77).
15	
16	Phase guard:
17	  - Exploratory phase: access train_ids + val_ids only
18	  - Confirmatory phase: access test_ids — only after Gate 2 approval
19	  - Agents check state["gate_status"]["gate_2"].decision == "approved" before
20	    touching test split. Violation is a SAP violation.
21	
22	EXP_ prefix convention:
23	  - All exploratory artifacts use EXP_ filename prefix
24	  - No p-value decision language in EXP_ outputs
25	  - Confirmatory scripts carry a SAP header with hypothesis ID and decision rule
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
31	from typing import Any, Literal
32	
33	from ..state import HCGRCState
34	
35	# ── Shared input types ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	
37	class STRMMapping:
38	    """
39	    A single STRM mapping between two SCF controls.
40	
41	    In Phase 0 (synthetic): populated with placeholder values.
42	    In Phase 1+: populated from data/splits/*.parquet.
43	    """
44	    __slots__ = (
45	        "source_control_id", "target_control_id",
46	        "relationship_type", "strength_score",
47	        "source_domain", "target_domain",
48	        "source_framework", "target_framework",
49	    )
50	
51	    def __init__(
52	        self,
53	        source_control_id: str,
54	        target_control_id: str,
55	        relationship_type: Literal["subset", "superset", "intersection", "equal", "no_relation"],
56	        strength_score: float | None = None,
57	        source_domain: str | None = None,
58	        target_domain: str | None = None,
59	        source_framework: str | None = None,
60	        target_framework: str | None = None,
61	    ) -> None:
62	        self.source_control_id = source_control_id
63	        self.target_control_id = target_control_id
64	        self.relationship_type = relationship_type
65	        self.strength_score = strength_score
66	        self.source_domain = source_domain
67	        self.target_domain = target_domain
68	        self.source_framework = source_framework
69	        self.target_framework = target_framework
70	
71	
72	class ControlMetadata:
73	    """Metadata for a single SCF control node."""
74	    __slots__ = (
75	        "control_id", "domain", "framework",
76	        "maturity_level", "mcr_dsr",
77	    )
78	
79	    def __init__(
80	        self,
81	        control_id: str,
82	        domain: str,
83	        framework: str,
84	        maturity_level: str | None = None,
85	        mcr_dsr: Literal["MCR", "DSR", None] = None,
86	    ) -> None:
87	        self.control_id = control_id
88	        self.domain = domain
89	        self.framework = framework
90	        self.maturity_level = maturity_level
91	        self.mcr_dsr = mcr_dsr
92	
93	
94	# ── Phase guard ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
95	
96	class SAPViolationError(Exception):
97	    """Raised when an agent attempts to access test data before Gate 2."""
98	    pass
99	
100

100	
101	def assert_gate2_approved(state: HCGRCState) -> None:
102	    """
103	    Raise SAPViolationError if Gate 2 has not been approved.
104	
105	    Must be called before any agent accesses test_ids or test split data.
106	    This is the primary enforcement mechanism for the pre-registration firewall.
107	    """
108	    gate_status = state.get("gate_status", {})
109	    gate_2 = gate_status.get("gate_2")
110	    if gate_2 is None or gate_2.get("decision") != "approved":
111	        raise SAPViolationError(
112	            "Attempted to access test split before Gate 2 approval. "
113	            "This is a SAP violation. Gate 2 status: "
114	            f"{gate_2.get('decision') if gate_2 else 'not_run'}. "
115	            "Do not access test_ids or confirmatory split until gate_2.decision == 'approved'."
116	        )
117	
118	
119	# ── Base agent ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
120	
121	class BaseResearchAgent(ABC):
122	    """
123	    Abstract base for all HC-GRC Phase 1 research agents.
124	
125	    Subclasses implement run_exploratory() and run_confirmatory() separately.
126	    The __call__ method dispatches based on state["phase"].
127	
128	    Protected agents set PROTECTED = True — checked by Agent Evolution before
129	    any prompt modification.
130	    """
131	
132	    PROTECTED: bool = False  # override in P1–P5, statistical-analyst, hypothesis-formalizer
133	    AGENT_ID: str = ""       # kebab-case, matches AGENT.md name field
134	    # Set True once the agent's run_* methods are real. While False, a
135	    # NotImplementedError is an expected Phase-0/1 stub; once True, a
136	    # NotImplementedError is a genuine bug the orchestrator must not silently
137	    # swallow as 'stub_pending' (#202).
138	    IMPLEMENTED: bool = False
139	
140	    def __call__(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
141	        """
142	        LangGraph-compatible call: returns a partial state update dict.
143	
144	        Dispatches to run_exploratory() or run_confirmatory() based on phase.
145	
146	        The canonical phase values written by the graph are phase_0 / phase_1 /
147	        phase_2 (see state.Phase). Exploratory analysis runs in phase_0/phase_1;
148	        confirmatory analysis runs in phase_2, where the Gate 2 SAP firewall
149	        (assert_gate2_approved) must hold before any test-split access. The legacy
150	        string aliases "exploratory"/"confirmatory" are accepted defensively.
151	        (Hardening pass 1, #17: phase_2 previously hit the else-branch and raised,
152	        so run_confirmatory and the SAP firewall were unreachable.)
153	        """
154	        phase = state.get("phase", "phase_0")
155	        if phase in ("phase_0", "phase_1", "exploratory"):
156	            return self.run_exploratory(state)
157	        elif phase in ("phase_2", "confirmatory"):
158	            assert_gate2_approved(state)
159	            return self.run_confirmatory(state)
160	        else:
161	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown phase: {phase!r}")
162	
163	    @abstractmethod
164	    def run_exploratory(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
165	        """
166	        Execute exploratory analysis on train + val splits.
167	
168	        All output artifact filenames must be prefixed EXP_.
169	        No p-value decision language in any exploratory output.
170	        Must not touch test_ids.
171	        """
172	        ...
173	
174	    @abstractmethod
175	    def run_confirmatory(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
176	        """
177	        Execute confirmatory analysis on test split for one hypothesis.
178	
179	        Gate 2 approval is verified by __call__ before this is invoked.
180	        Every confirmatory script must carry a SAP header:
181	          # SAP header: hypothesis_id=H[x].[n], test=[named test],
182	          #             effect_size_threshold=[value], decision_rule=[criterion]
183	        """
184	        ...
185	
186	
187	# ── Base pipeline agent ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
188	
189	class BasePipelineAgent(ABC):
190	    """
191	    Abstract base for data pipeline agents (acquisition, curation, steward, embedding).
192	
193	    Pipeline agents prepare and version the research corpus. They do not perform
194	    exploratory or confirmatory analysis — they have no run_exploratory() /
195	    run_confirmatory() distinction. They run once at project initialization and
196	    re-run when the SCF corpus is updated.
197	
198	    Pipeline agents are never PROTECTED — they contain no analysis logic that
199	    could affect research conclusions. Agent Evolution may modify them without
200	    Escalation approval.
201	
202	    The run() return dict is a partial HCGRCState update, merged by the graph runtime.
203	    Agents must never mutate state in place.
204	    """
205	
206	    AGENT_ID: str = ""     # kebab-case, matches AGENT.md name field
207	    PROTECTED: bool = False  # never True for pipeline agents
208	    IMPLEMENTED: bool = False  # see BaseResearchAgent.IMPLEMENTED (#202)
209	
210	    @abstractmethod
211	    def run(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
212	        """
213	        Execute the pipeline stage and return a partial HCGRCState update.
214	
215	        Raises NotImplementedError until the real SCF data pipeline is live
216	        on the Mac mini with DVC and Qdrant configured.
217	        """
218	        ...
219

content.text: Perfect. Now I have the complete picture. Let me verify the comment at the top of phase1.py one more time to be absolutely certain about how the pipeline documents itself:

content.content:
1	"""
2	Phase 1 LangGraph node wrappers.
3	
4	Topology (Phase 1 exploratory subgraph):
5	
6	  phase_1_entry
7	      ↓
8	  data_pipeline          ← DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent
9	      ↓                    → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent (sequential)
10	  exploratory_phase      ← P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 → P5 (sequential now;
11	      ↓                    TODO: parallelize via Send() when implementations live)
12	  hypothesis_formalize   ← HypothesisFormalizerAgent
13	      ↓
14	  gate_2                 ← (in gates.py)
15	      ↓
16	  [approved: confirmatory_entry | rejected: exploratory_phase loop]
17	
18	Parallelization note:
19	  P1-P5 are currently called sequentially within exploratory_phase_node.
20	  When implementations are live, replace with Send()-based fan-out:
21	    def dispatch_exploratory(state) -> list[Send]:
22	        return [Send("p1_node", state), Send("p2_node", state), ...]
23	  Each worker appends to eda_artifacts and eda_agent_statuses via append reducers.
24	  After all workers complete, route to hypothesis_formalize.
25	  See LangGraph map-reduce pattern for the fan-in wiring.
26	
27	NotImplementedError handling:
28	  Each P1-P5 call is wrapped in try/except NotImplementedError. Agents that
29	  have not been implemented yet record a "stub_pending" status to eda_agent_statuses.
30	  This allows the graph to execute in tests and governance dry-runs without
31	  requiring all research implementations to be live.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have all the evidence I need. The docstring at lines 8-9 of phase1.py shows the wrong order too.

## Summary of Findings

**VERIFIED DEFECT CONFIRMED**

The candidate claim is **correct and this is a genuine critical bug** — but let me break down the precise verification:

### Evidence Chain:

1. **Code location (src/nodes/phase1.py:145-150)** - The stages list executes agents in this order:
   - DataAcquisitionAgent → **DataCurationAgent → DataStewardAgent** → EmbeddingAgent

2. **Specification requirement (agents/02-data/data-curation/AGENT.md:22)** states explicitly:
   > "Runs after Data Steward validation."

3. **Specification diagram (lines 24-34)** shows:
   ```
   [Data Steward: validated splits]
           ↓
   [Data Curation Agent]
   ```

4. **Input contract (agents/02-data/data-curation/AGENT.md:40)** declares:
   - Input: "Validated splits" from "data/splits/" with note "Train/val/test from Data Steward"

5. **Agent implementation (src/agents/data_curation/agent.py:67)** confirms in docstring:
   > "Apply curation rules to validated splits from DataStewardAgent"

6. **Code topology docstring (src/nodes/phase1.py:8-9)** also documents the wrong order:
   ```
   data_pipeline          ← DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent
                            → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent (sequential)
   ```

### Critical Issue:

Both agents are **Phase 1 stubs** (`IMPLEMENTED = False`; both `.run()` methods raise `NotImplementedError` at lines 87 and 106 respectively). The pipeline catches `NotImplementedError` and records `stub_pending` status (phase1.py:179), so currently the graph continues safely when it hits the first unimplemented agent.

**However**, the ordering defect is REAL and will become a critical safety issue when both agents are implemented:
- DataCurationAgent expects validated splits (per spec and its own docstring)
- Running curation before steward means curation receives **unvalidated raw data**
- This violates the spec-documented data governance pipeline

### Severity Adjustment:

The candidate marked this as **critical**, which is appropriate for when these agents are implemented. However, **is_stub_by_design must be FALSE** because:
- This is NOT an intentional stub (NotImplementedError is stub, but the ordering is active implementation logic)
- The ordering bug exists in fully-implemented code (the stages list and loop in phase1.py are not stubs)
- The defect will silently cause wrong behavior once the agents are implemented

input.title: Pipeline stage ordering misalignment with AGENT.md spec
input.verification: DEFECT CONFIRMED. Code at src/nodes/phase1.py:145-150 executes DataCurationAgent before DataStewardAgent, contradicting agents/02-data/data-curation/AGENT.md line 22 ("Runs after Data Steward validation") and the workflow diagram (lines 24-34) showing steward-validated splits flowing TO curation. The spec input contract (line 40) explicitly requires "Validated splits" from Data Steward. Both agent implementations confirm this expectation (src/agents/data_curation/agent.py:67 docstring states "validated splits from DataStewardAgent"). The stages list in phase1.py is fully implemented orchestration logic (not a stub), while the agents themselves are Phase 1 stubs. The ordering defect is latent and will cause data integrity violations once agents are implemented — curation will receive unvalidated raw data instead of steward-validated splits.
input.recommended_action: Reorder the stages list in data_pipeline_node (src/nodes/phase1.py:145-150) to place DataStewardAgent before DataCurationAgent: [(acquisition, 'data-acquisition'), (steward, 'data-steward'), (curation, 'data-curation'), (embedding, 'embedding-agent')]. Also correct the topology diagram in the module docstring (lines 8-9) to reflect the correct order. This fix must be completed before Phase 1 agent implementations go live, as the current ordering will cause silent data governance violations.
